programmes.show_description: Mechanical expert Steve gets a vintage pinball machine working again. Plus, Lucia and Dominic work together on a nose cone from a 1960s drag racing bike.
Around the world, there are extraordinary building mistakes. Design teams and experts analyse what went wrong and how engineers are fixing them.
More incredible images of destruction as catastrophic flooding topples a hotel, a passenger falls out of a hot air balloon and dozens of chemical-filled drums burst into flames
Witness two cranes collapsing at a construction site, a spectacular crash on a notorious ski run and a massive firework explosion that smashes windows.
Ron Pitts reveals more incredible true stories of destruction including diners hit by a surprise tornado and a raft of bus crashes caught on camera.
Ron Pitts puts you at the scene of incredible destruction. Watch as nine gallons of chemicals suddenly burst into flames and a motorcycle without breaks slams into a crowd.
More everyday items go under the microscope. How are common objects including digital paintings, sundae cups and badminton rackets made?
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production processes. How are objects including dumplings and brass faucets made?
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
The experts delicately restore a clay keepsake of a baby's footprints, bring the music back to a salon radio and repair a beloved childhood teddy.
Modern day treasure hunter Drew Pritchard travels the length and breadth of the UK in search of weird and wonderful objects. The bigger the hoard, the happier he is.
Modern day treasure hunter Drew Pritchard travels the length and breadth of the UK in search of weird and wonderful objects. The bigger the hoard, the happier he is.
Kellie and Henri unleash a new piece of tech on a vast salt lake. And in a remote frontier town, a chance meeting could turn a crisis into a jackpot for lone wolf Rick Fishers.
On the verge of a huge payday, the Dirt Dogs face up to a possible disaster, while Ted and Lecky's mission to find a one-in-a million nugget turns into a manhunt.
The Tunnel Rats face an unexploded bomb and deadly gasses. The Bros attempt to rescue their season, and rookie team The Poms go underground for the first time.
Boulder Boys Aaron and Ron almost lose their $60,000 excavator on unstable ground. Off-the-grid opal hunting family The McFarlanes race a storm in Lightning Ridge.
The experts delicately restore a clay keepsake of a baby's footprints, bring the music back to a salon radio and repair a beloved childhood teddy.
From Bodmin To Oxfordshire. Henry and Sam are in Bodmin Moor rummaging through a collection built up over 25 years. It includes amphibious craft, motorbikes, nuts and bolts.
Redruth. In Redruth, Henry and mechanic Sam are confronted with sheds filled with ramshackle contents, from Harry Potter's Anglia to a model canary-yellow Lamborghini.
Kevin / Lincolnshire. Henry and Fuzz head to Lincolnshire to see Kevin's collection of vintage lorries and trucks. They find a Cornish fire engine, but is it too hot to handle?
Francis / Oxfordshire. Henry and Fuzz are staying local, visiting Francis' farm in Oxfordshire. There's tractors, bikes and trinkets, but what will they come away with?
Action Stations! A battalion of Action Man toys, an old RAF jacket, and a Second World War sweetheart brooch end up in a bidding war as they go under Angus's gavel.
Seaside To Stateside. Angus tries to fund a couple's move to the US by selling their precious gemstones and retro video games, and later unearths some pricey 70s furniture.
Student Takes On Master. Debut road tripper Hettie Jago takes on regular dealer David Harper. The pair start in Scotland with £1500 each and a 1980s Mercedes 280 SL.
A Close Encounter With A Cow. Auctioneer Hettie Jago takes on dealer David Harper. The second leg of their trip takes them to Glasgow and the Scottish Borders.
Paul Webb. Henry and Fuzz visit Paul in the Malvern Hills, a man with a perfectly maintained collection of Japanese motorbikes including a Honda 750 Four.
Frank. Henry and Fuzz are in Moretonhampstead at Frank's motoring museum - home to hundreds of cars, bikes and rare pieces of motoring memorabilia.
Bright Ideas. Guz transforms his S2000 with new lights and under glow, jumps into a turbo Micra thrill ride, and explores bold builds from across the modding world.
Dara Ó Briain introduces topical chat and laugh out loud games with guests Rhys James, Ed Byrne, Glenn Moore, Sara Pascoe and Emmanuel Sonubi.
MotoGP: Thailand Highlights. Highlights of all the action from the MotoGP 2026 motorcycle racing World Championship at the Buriram International Circuit in Thailand.
How do they put the holes in Swiss cheese? How do they restore and improve a classic battle tank? And how do robotic librarians run one of the world's biggest book collections?
How do they persuade an oyster to grow a perfect pearl? How do they put the twist in classic Pennsylvania pretzels? And how do they build Venice's traditional gondolas?
Ford XR8 - Australia. Mike and Elvis engage in the ultimate Aussie showdown: Ford versus Holden. Can their Ford XR8 outpace the Holden GTS at the Mount Panorama circuit?
VW Kombi - Australia. Mike and Elvis find a 1970s classic VW camper in need of some mechanical TLC. Can they turn it into a long-range camper fit for the Australian wilderness?
From Bodmin To Oxfordshire. Henry and Sam are in Bodmin Moor rummaging through a collection built up over 25 years. It includes amphibious craft, motorbikes, nuts and bolts.
Redruth. In Redruth, Henry and mechanic Sam are confronted with sheds filled with ramshackle contents, from Harry Potter's Anglia to a model canary-yellow Lamborghini.
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their unique production processes. How are objects including saxophones made?
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as 3D cups and stainless-steel beer kegs made?
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Amanda and Julie are tasked with restoring a toy horse that has huge sentimental value. Meanwhile, Steve and Will repair a penny arcade game.
In Suffolk, Drew picks up a collection of mid-century snout sticks originally used in pig auctions. Next, Drew and Tee spot gothic treasure in a Swansea film studio.
Drew and Tee head to the former East Germany, birthplace of early modern design. They visit a fair full of Bauhaus brilliance in Berlin and one of Europe's biggest flea markets.
As lone wolf, Rick Fishers, teams up with a gold field legend, Ted and Lecky uncover a bounty of historic finds. Meanwhile, the Gold Gypsies embark on high risk mission.
As bushland miners The Dirt Dogs encounter a possible find of a lifetime, Henri and Kellie battle brutal weather and each other. And the Gold Gypsies strike a deadly threat.
Desperate for a find, the Bushmen gamble on a dangerous mining method deep below ground. In Lightning Ridge an injury causes The Bros a serious setback.
The Young Guns' claim is ravaged by a vast dust storm. Meanwhile, the Boulder Boys chase old leads and the Bushmen face a disaster fifteen metres underground.
Amanda and Julie are tasked with restoring a toy horse that has huge sentimental value. Meanwhile, Steve and Will repair a penny arcade game.
Hampshire. Presenter Henry Cole and motorcycle restorer Sam Lovegrove scour the UK's sheds for hidden vintage automotive treasures to fix up and sell on.
In The Heart Of Oxfordshire. Henry and Sam are in the heart of Oxfordshire where they find rare Minis, 1970s Rollers, and a pristine rally car - but can they get her running?
Mike / Somerset. Henry and Fuzz are down in Somerset visiting Mike's farm. Will they play it safe with a motorbike or take a risk on something agricultural?
Rebecca And Sue / Nottinghamshire. The pursuit of profit sees Henry and Fuzz rummaging through Rebecca and Sue's sheds near Newark. It's home to all manner of vehicles and parts.
Pricey Mice In The Saleroom. Brothers sell their late mum's furniture. And a couple are overwhelmed by inherited heirlooms. But will a former lover's engagement ring sell?
Cambodia Or Bust. Angus sells a woman's possessions to fund her retirement in Cambodia. And he helps a widower part with the treasures he shared with his late wife.
It's All Kicking Off. Hettie, David and their 1980s Mercedes 280 SL are in Cumbria. But who will strike a decisive blow in their best-of-five auction contest?
Horses Crabs And Sausages. Hettie and David hope that their 1980s Mercedes SL 280 convertible will cope with the challenging roads of the 'District' as they hunt for antiques.
Sporting Trials Car. Henry and Fuzz find a rare custom IRIS Sporting Trials Car. It's irresistible for the boys who get to work returning it to its former glory.
H Is For Help! With the workshop haemorrhaging money, Richard brings in help. A merchandising scheme backfires when Lewis massively over-orders stock.
Bright Ideas. Guz transforms his S2000 with new lights and under glow, jumps into a turbo Micra thrill ride, and explores bold builds from across the modding world.
Japanese scientists stimulate oysters to form perfect pearls. Handcrafted pretzels are made with a twist. Workers maintain the craft of the gondolier.
Roulette wheels are made to be perfectly unpredictable. The secrets behind the purest olive oil are revealed. An old wreck is made into the Batmobile.
